title: Marine Le Pen decides to run for president after appeals convition
subhead: The far-right leader said she would appeal to France's highest court after her embezzlement conviction was upheld but her ban on holding elected office was shortened, allowing her to stand in the 2027 election.

Pyrénées wildfires destroy everything in their path: 'I didn't think I'd make it out alive'
subhead: The residents of Ille-sur-Têt, a town that was evacuated, remain in shock as the Trévillach wildfire – the largest the region has seen in 50 years – was still not under control on Monday evening.

title: In Quebec, 'moving day' overshadowed by housing crisis
subhead: A unique tradition in the Canadian province, tens of thousands of people move on the same day, July 1. But in recent years, this ritual has been marred by serious difficulties in finding new housing.

title: 2026 World Cup: Switzerland beat Colombia on penalties to reach quarter-finals
subhead: Switzerland beat Colombia 4-3 on penalties after a goalless draw on Tuesday. The Swiss will face Argentina in Kansas City on Saturday after Lionel Messi's team earlier beat Egypt 3-2 in a wild game in Atlanta.

title: Book reveals Trump's improvisation and concentration of power
subhead: Two journalists from the 'New York Times' retrace the early months of the US president's second term, highlighting the ruptures and upheavals his approach to power has unleashed.

subhead: A study by scientists and journalists from the Forever Pollution Project, coordinated by Le Monde, reveals that cleanup efforts are largely ineffective and highlights the urgent need for strict limits on emissions.

title: One hundred early works by Claude Monet reunited at Le Havre's André Malraux Museum
subhead: The MuMa exhibition, showcasing drawings, caricatures and paintings, places the painter in his family and regional context.

title: Netanyahu government's legal coup challenges Israel's justice system: 'The final nail in the coffin of democracy'
subhead: By declaring he would not comply with a Supreme Court ruling, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is directly confronting the country's highest legal authority, plunging Israel into a constitutional crisis.
